semanticActions.intent: Load the CLI-created run manifest for the refactor lab.
Execute the original Node script against safe fixtures before transformation.
Materialize behavior inventory, semantic target authority candidate, thin facade, and review package.
Emit receipts proving characterization and any semantic translation blocker.

behaviorInventory.description: Compare required document names against supplied document names.
Block SOC 2 compliant claim when SOC 2 Type II Report is missing.
Return blocked or passed status, blocker, missing documents, unsupported claims, and next action.

strategy: characterize original output against safe fixture
add missing generic operations to the conveyor runtime surface as data contracts
block replacement until runtime-surface operations are bound and before/after equivalence passes
content.noManualAssemblyRule: Run outputs are materialized by the node-script-to-sej-refactor SOG through the CLI.

content.completionPosture: The refactor lane adds the missing operations as data-driven runtime-surface authority and keeps script replacement blocked until they execute and equivalence passes.

content.inputContract: vendor evidence packet with vendorName, documents, claims, and requiredDocuments
content.outputContract: readiness result with status, blocker, vendorName, checklist, missingDocuments, unsupportedClaims, nextAction

content.equivalenceRequirement: after-output must match before-output before facade promotion

content.completionPosture: Semantic authority candidate is extracted and the required runtime-surface operations are declared as data contracts, but execution and facade promotion remain blocked until they are bound and before/after equivalence passes.
